records.ety: [L. convulsus, p. p. of convellere to tear up, to shake; con- + vellere to pluck, pull.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Convulsed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Convulsing.]

defs: To contract violently and irregularly, as the muscular parts of an animal body; to shake with irregular spasms, as in excessive laughter, or in agony from grief or pain.
quotes: With emotions which checked his voice and convulsed his powerful frame.

defs: To agitate greatly; to shake violently.
quotes: The world is convulsed by the agonies of great nations.
